setting.note: Roa Bastos maps the Paraguayan people across half a century, from the red-earth villages of the central heartland to the waterless scrub of the Chaco. Interlinked stories move between Itapé and its wood-carver, the railway workshops and sugar country around Sapukai, the capital Asunción, and the far western battlefields of the Chaco War against Bolivia. The map runs wide, from the Guairá hills east of the capital out across the river to the northern Chaco.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Augusto Roa Bastos, first published 1960 as Hijo de hombre. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: Son of Man, Augusto Roa Bastos (1960). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The red earth of the Paraguayan heartland and the rust of the abandoned railway. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
